Tests the behavior of the `charAt` method on a string when the index is out of bounds.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that uses an assertion to verify the behavior of the `charAt` method of JavaScript's `String` object. It checks that calling `charAt(3)` on the string `"abc"` returns an empty string `""`, which is the expected result when the index is out of the string's bounds. The script serves to confirm that the `charAt` method correctly handles cases where the specified index is greater than the length of the string. This is a narrow functionality test focusing on a specific aspect of the `String` object.
